title: phaseolus vulgaris var. oreolinus
intro: Phaseolus vulgaris var. oreolinus, also known as the kidney bean, is a variety of the common bean that has been cultivated for its nutritional and medicinal properties. This plant is known for its high fiber and protein content, as well as its rich antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The kidney bean has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, high blood pressure, and inflammation, and is also believed to have anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, kidney beans can be consumed as a dried powder, tea, or cooked as a part of traditional dishes, and are also used in some folk remedies to promote digestive health and reduce the risk of chronic diseases.
